one_time_description: Debt adjustment on demand loans (Bridging Finance write-down from ~$113M to $25M)
economic_notes: First Nation operates multiple business enterprises including pharmacy, one-stop shops, tobacco store, and gaming operations. Ongoing loan dispute with receiver of Bridging Finance Inc. regarding $113M principal plus $56M interest claim. Jordan's Principle entity not consolidated due to unavailability of accounting records.
notes: Qualified audit opinion with multiple bases: (1) unresolved receivables from Chief Peguis Investment Corporation fully allowanced at $20.3M; (2) $25M provision for Bridging Finance loan settlement with disputed amounts; (3) Jordan's Principle entity not consolidated due to unavailable records; (4) no budget comparison included contrary to PSAS. Going concern uncertainty noted due to debt servicing requirements. Prior year included $111M one-time debt adjustment gain and $22.25M bad debt write-off on CPIC receivables. Total ISC revenue per reconciliation note is $94,671,041 combining ISC ($80,018,690) and FNIH ($14,652,351). Province of Manitoba funding $2,250,032 included in total government transfers. CMHC $386,462 included in total government transfers. ESDC $2,425,566 included. Canadian Heritage $333,880 included. RCMP Housing $67,200 included. OSR calculated as total revenue minus all government transfers (ISC+FNHI+CMHC+ESDC+CanHeritage+RCMP Housing+Province of MB).
